This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun |image =[[File:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (circular rear on the right, rectangular front on the left). |affiliation = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Shibutani Town, Nara Prefecture{{Sfn|Keikō Emperor Mausoleum Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 3.00 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 56.28 |iso = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Length: 300 m
Height: 25 m (rear circular part) built = Mid-to-late 4th century (circa 321–340 AD[[Amano Sueki]] "Considering the Tombs and burials of the Mausoleum" in *The Forefront of Ancient History Research: Mausoleums*, Yōsensha, 2016, pp. 61-62.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/koramukodaikaranomemessezi/wanogoozidai/10289.html builts of Giant Kofuns 1 (No. 162)] – Fujidera City Website). |burial = Unknown |burial_person = Designated as Emperor Keikō by the Imperial Household Agency |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (reported) stone pillow |mausoleum = Designated "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 8th largest Kofun in Japan / 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas in Japan] – Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018.
Largest Kofun from the early Kofun period |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Shibutani Mukoyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Keyhole-shaped mound-west }} '''Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun''' (渋谷向山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. While the actual burial figure is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" (山邊道上陵), the tomb of Emperor Keikō. This is the 8th largest Kofun in Japan and the 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture, dating back to the mid-to-late 4th century. Recent studies suggest a slightly earlier construction date in the mid-4th century. == Overview == The Kofun is located along the eastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a slope extending westward from Mount Ryuo. The {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe-no-Michi|qq=}} trail passes near the rear circular portion of the mound, and remnants of ancient stone-paved paths lead toward {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}. It is managed by the Imperial Household Agency, and multiple investigations have been conducted by the agency’s Shoryobu Division since 1971, as well as academic studies in 2016. The keyhole-shaped mound faces westward (downhill). The rear circular section comprises four tiers, and the rectangular front comprises three tiers. Measuring 300 meters in length, the Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun is the largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and the second-largest in Nara Prefecture after the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|lt=Maruyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}} in Kashihara (310 meters). A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun |image =[[File:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (circular rear on the right, rectangular front on the left). |affiliation = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Shibutani Town, Nara Prefecture{{Sfn|Keikō Emperor Mausoleum Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 3.00 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 56.28 |iso = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Length: 300 m
Height: 25 m (rear circular part) built = Mid-to-late 4th century (circa 321–340 AD[[Amano Sueki]] "Considering the Tombs and burials of the Mausoleum" in *The Forefront of Ancient History Research: Mausoleums*, Yōsensha, 2016, pp. 61-62.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/koramukodaikaranomemessezi/wanogoozidai/10289.html builts of Giant Kofuns 1 (No. 162)] – Fujidera City Website). |burial = Unknown |burial_person = Designated as Emperor Keikō by the Imperial Household Agency |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (reported) stone pillow |mausoleum = Designated "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 8th largest Kofun in Japan / 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas in Japan] – Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018.
Largest Kofun from the early Kofun period |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Shibutani Mukoyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Keyhole-shaped mound-west }} '''Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun''' (渋谷向山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. While the actual burial figure is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" (山邊道上陵), the tomb of Emperor Keikō. This is the 8th largest Kofun in Japan and the 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture, dating back to the mid-to-late 4th century. Recent studies suggest a slightly earlier construction date in the mid-4th century. == Overview == The Kofun is located along the eastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a slope extending westward from Mount Ryuo. The {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe-no-Michi|qq=}} trail passes near the rear circular portion of the mound, and remnants of ancient stone-paved paths lead toward {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}. It is managed by the Imperial Household Agency, and multiple investigations have been conducted by the agency’s Shoryobu Division since 1971, as well as academic studies in 2016. The keyhole-shaped mound faces westward (downhill). The rear circular section comprises four tiers, and the rectangular front comprises three tiers. Measuring 300 meters in length, the Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun is the largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and the second-largest in Nara Prefecture after the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|lt=Maruyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}} in Kashihara (310 meters). A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|渋谷向山古墳|version=104662227|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Yanagimoto Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausolea of Japan]] [[Category:Yamanobe-no-Michi]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun]][[sn:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun]] [[ja:渋谷向山古墳]] [[sn:hibutani Mukoyama Kofun]] While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated Full view of the mound (circular rear on the right, rectangular front on the left). |affiliation = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Shibutani Town, Nara Prefecture{{Sfn|Keikō Emperor Mausoleum Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 3.00 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 56.28 |iso = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Length: 300 m
Height: 25 m (rear circular part) built = Mid-to-late 4th century (circa 321–340 AD[[Amano Sueki]] "Considering the Tombs and burials of the Mausoleum" in *The Forefront of Ancient History Research: Mausoleums*, Yōsensha, 2016, pp. 61-62.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/koramukodaikaranomemessezi/wanogoozidai/10289.html builts of Giant Kofuns 1 (No. 162)] – Fujidera City Website). |burial = Unknown |burial_person = Designated as Emperor Keikō by the Imperial Household Agency |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (reported) stone pillow |mausoleum = Designated "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 8th largest Kofun in Japan / 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas in Japan] – Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018.
Largest Kofun from the early Kofun period |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Shibutani Mukoyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Keyhole-shaped mound-west }} '''Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun''' (渋谷向山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. While the actual burial figure is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" (山邊道上陵), the tomb of Emperor Keikō. This is the 8th largest Kofun in Japan and the 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture, dating back to the mid-to-late 4th century. Recent studies suggest a slightly earlier construction date in the mid-4th century. == Overview == The Kofun is located along the eastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a slope extending westward from Mount Ryuo. The {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe-no-Michi|qq=}} trail passes near the rear circular portion of the mound, and remnants of ancient stone-paved paths lead toward {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}. It is managed by the Imperial Household Agency, and multiple investigations have been conducted by the agency’s Shoryobu Division since 1971, as well as academic studies in 2016. The keyhole-shaped mound faces westward (downhill). The rear circular section comprises four tiers, and the rectangular front comprises three tiers. Measuring 300 meters in length, the Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun is the largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and the second-largest in Nara Prefecture after the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|lt=Maruyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}} in Kashihara (310 meters). A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated Full view of the mound (circular rear on the right, rectangular front on the left). |affiliation = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Shibutani Town, Nara Prefecture{{Sfn|Keikō Emperor Mausoleum Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 3.00 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 56.28 |iso = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Length: 300 m
Height: 25 m (rear circular part) built = Mid-to-late 4th century (circa 321–340 AD[[Amano Sueki]] "Considering the Tombs and burials of the Mausoleum" in *The Forefront of Ancient History Research: Mausoleums*, Yōsensha, 2016, pp. 61-62.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/koramukodaikaranomemessezi/wanogoozidai/10289.html builts of Giant Kofuns 1 (No. 162)] – Fujidera City Website). |burial = Unknown |burial_person = Designated as Emperor Keikō by the Imperial Household Agency |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (reported) stone pillow |mausoleum = Designated "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 8th largest Kofun in Japan / 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas in Japan] – Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018.
Largest Kofun from the early Kofun period |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Shibutani Mukoyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Keyhole-shaped mound-west }} '''Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun''' (渋谷向山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. While the actual burial figure is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" (山邊道上陵), the tomb of Emperor Keikō. This is the 8th largest Kofun in Japan and the 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture, dating back to the mid-to-late 4th century. Recent studies suggest a slightly earlier construction date in the mid-4th century. == Overview == The Kofun is located along the eastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a slope extending westward from Mount Ryuo. The {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe-no-Michi|qq=}} trail passes near the rear circular portion of the mound, and remnants of ancient stone-paved paths lead toward {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}. It is managed by the Imperial Household Agency, and multiple investigations have been conducted by the agency’s Shoryobu Division since 1971, as well as academic studies in 2016. The keyhole-shaped mound faces westward (downhill). The rear circular section comprises four tiers, and the rectangular front comprises three tiers. Measuring 300 meters in length, the Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun is the largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and the second-largest in Nara Prefecture after the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|lt=Maruyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}} in Kashihara (310 meters). A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|渋谷向山古墳|version=104662227|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Yanagimoto Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausolea of Japan]] While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated Full view of the mound (circular rear on the right, rectangular front on the left). |affiliation = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Shibutani Town, Nara Prefecture{{Sfn|Keikō Emperor Mausoleum Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 3.00 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 56.28 |iso = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Length: 300 m
Height: 25 m (rear circular part) built = Mid-to-late 4th century (circa 321–340 AD[[Amano Sueki]] "Considering the Tombs and burials of the Mausoleum" in *The Forefront of Ancient History Research: Mausoleums*, Yōsensha, 2016, pp. 61-62.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/koramukodaikaranomemessezi/wanogoozidai/10289.html builts of Giant Kofuns 1 (No. 162)] – Fujidera City Website). |burial = Unknown |burial_person = Designated as Emperor Keikō by the Imperial Household Agency |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (reported) stone pillow |mausoleum = Designated "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 8th largest Kofun in Japan / 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas in Japan] – Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018.
Largest Kofun from the early Kofun period |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Shibutani Mukoyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Keyhole-shaped mound-west }} '''Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun''' (渋谷向山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. While the actual burial figure is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" (山邊道上陵), the tomb of Emperor Keikō. This is the 8th largest Kofun in Japan and the 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture, dating back to the mid-to-late 4th century. Recent studies suggest a slightly earlier construction date in the mid-4th century. == Overview == The Kofun is located along the eastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a slope extending westward from Mount Ryuo. The {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe-no-Michi|qq=}} trail passes near the rear circular portion of the mound, and remnants of ancient stone-paved paths lead toward {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}. It is managed by the Imperial Household Agency, and multiple investigations have been conducted by the agency’s Shoryobu Division since 1971, as well as academic studies in 2016. The keyhole-shaped mound faces westward (downhill). The rear circular section comprises four tiers, and the rectangular front comprises three tiers. Measuring 300 meters in length, the Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun is the largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and the second-largest in Nara Prefecture after the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|lt=Maruyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}} in Kashihara (310 meters). A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated Full view of the mound (circular rear on the right, rectangular front on the left). |affiliation = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Shibutani Town, Nara Prefecture{{Sfn|Keikō Emperor Mausoleum Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 3.00 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 56.28 |iso = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Length: 300 m
Height: 25 m (rear circular part) built = Mid-to-late 4th century (circa 321–340 AD[[Amano Sueki]] "Considering the Tombs and burials of the Mausoleum" in *The Forefront of Ancient History Research: Mausoleums*, Yōsensha, 2016, pp. 61-62.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/koramukodaikaranomemessezi/wanogoozidai/10289.html builts of Giant Kofuns 1 (No. 162)] – Fujidera City Website). |burial = Unknown |burial_person = Designated as Emperor Keikō by the Imperial Household Agency |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (reported) stone pillow |mausoleum = Designated "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 8th largest Kofun in Japan / 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas in Japan] – Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018.
Largest Kofun from the early Kofun period |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Shibutani Mukoyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Keyhole-shaped mound-west }} '''Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun''' (渋谷向山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. While the actual burial figure is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" (山邊道上陵), the tomb of Emperor Keikō. This is the 8th largest Kofun in Japan and the 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture, dating back to the mid-to-late 4th century. Recent studies suggest a slightly earlier construction date in the mid-4th century. == Overview == The Kofun is located along the eastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a slope extending westward from Mount Ryuo. The {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe-no-Michi|qq=}} trail passes near the rear circular portion of the mound, and remnants of ancient stone-paved paths lead toward {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}. It is managed by the Imperial Household Agency, and multiple investigations have been conducted by the agency’s Shoryobu Division since 1971, as well as academic studies in 2016. The keyhole-shaped mound faces westward (downhill). The rear circular section comprises four tiers, and the rectangular front comprises three tiers. Measuring 300 meters in length, the Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun is the largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and the second-largest in Nara Prefecture after the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|lt=Maruyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}} in Kashihara (310 meters). A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun |image =[[File:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (circular rear on the right, rectangular front on the left). |affiliation = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Shibutani Town, Nara Prefecture{{Sfn|Keikō Emperor Mausoleum Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 3.00 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 56.28 |iso = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Length: 300 m
Height: 25 m (rear circular part) built = Mid-to-late 4th century (circa 321–340 AD[[Amano Sueki]] "Considering the Tombs and burials of the Mausoleum" in *The Forefront of Ancient History Research: Mausoleums*, Yōsensha, 2016, pp. 61-62.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/koramukodaikaranomemessezi/wanogoozidai/10289.html builts of Giant Kofuns 1 (No. 162)] – Fujidera City Website). |burial = Unknown |burial_person = Designated as Emperor Keikō by the Imperial Household Agency |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (reported) stone pillow |mausoleum = Designated "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 8th largest Kofun in Japan / 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas in Japan] – Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018.
Largest Kofun from the early Kofun period |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Shibutani Mukoyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Keyhole-shaped mound-west }} '''Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun''' (渋谷向山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. While the actual burial figure is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" (山邊道上陵), the tomb of Emperor Keikō. This is the 8th largest Kofun in Japan and the 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture, dating back to the mid-to-late 4th century. Recent studies suggest a slightly earlier construction date in the mid-4th century. == Overview == The Kofun is located along the eastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a slope extending westward from Mount Ryuo. The {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe-no-Michi|qq=}} trail passes near the rear circular portion of the mound, and remnants of ancient stone-paved paths lead toward {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}. It is managed by the Imperial Household Agency, and multiple investigations have been conducted by the agency’s Shoryobu Division since 1971, as well as academic studies in 2016. The keyhole-shaped mound faces westward (downhill). The rear circular section comprises four tiers, and the rectangular front comprises three tiers. Measuring 300 meters in length, the Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun is the largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and the second-largest in Nara Prefecture after the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|lt=Maruyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}} in Kashihara (310 meters). A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun |image =[[File:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (circular rear on the right, rectangular front on the left). |affiliation = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Shibutani Town, Nara Prefecture{{Sfn|Keikō Emperor Mausoleum Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 3.00 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 56.28 |iso = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Length: 300 m
Height: 25 m (rear circular part) built = Mid-to-late 4th century (circa 321–340 AD[[Amano Sueki]] "Considering the Tombs and burials of the Mausoleum" in *The Forefront of Ancient History Research: Mausoleums*, Yōsensha, 2016, pp. 61-62.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/koramukodaikaranomemessezi/wanogoozidai/10289.html builts of Giant Kofuns 1 (No. 162)] – Fujidera City Website). |burial = Unknown |burial_person = Designated as Emperor Keikō by the Imperial Household Agency |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (reported) stone pillow |mausoleum = Designated "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 8th largest Kofun in Japan / 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas in Japan] – Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018.
Largest Kofun from the early Kofun period |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Shibutani Mukoyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Keyhole-shaped mound-west }} '''Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun''' (渋谷向山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. While the actual burial figure is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" (山邊道上陵), the tomb of Emperor Keikō. This is the 8th largest Kofun in Japan and the 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture, dating back to the mid-to-late 4th century. Recent studies suggest a slightly earlier construction date in the mid-4th century. == Overview == The Kofun is located along the eastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a slope extending westward from Mount Ryuo. The {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe-no-Michi|qq=}} trail passes near the rear circular portion of the mound, and remnants of ancient stone-paved paths lead toward {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}. It is managed by the Imperial Household Agency, and multiple investigations have been conducted by the agency’s Shoryobu Division since 1971, as well as academic studies in 2016. The keyhole-shaped mound faces westward (downhill). The rear circular section comprises four tiers, and the rectangular front comprises three tiers. Measuring 300 meters in length, the Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun is the largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and the second-largest in Nara Prefecture after the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|lt=Maruyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}} in Kashihara (310 meters). A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|渋谷向山古墳|version=104662227|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated Full view of the mound (circular rear on the right, rectangular front on the left). |affiliation = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Shibutani Town, Nara Prefecture{{Sfn|Keikō Emperor Mausoleum Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 3.00 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 56.28 |iso = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Length: 300 m
Height: 25 m (rear circular part) built = Mid-to-late 4th century (circa 321–340 AD[[Amano Sueki]] "Considering the Tombs and burials of the Mausoleum" in *The Forefront of Ancient History Research: Mausoleums*, Yōsensha, 2016, pp. 61-62.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/koramukodaikaranomemessezi/wanogoozidai/10289.html builts of Giant Kofuns 1 (No. 162)] – Fujidera City Website). |burial = Unknown |burial_person = Designated as Emperor Keikō by the Imperial Household Agency |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (reported) stone pillow |mausoleum = Designated "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 8th largest Kofun in Japan / 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas in Japan] – Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018.
Largest Kofun from the early Kofun period |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Shibutani Mukoyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Keyhole-shaped mound-west }} '''Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun''' (渋谷向山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. While the actual burial figure is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" (山邊道上陵), the tomb of Emperor Keikō. This is the 8th largest Kofun in Japan and the 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture, dating back to the mid-to-late 4th century. Recent studies suggest a slightly earlier construction date in the mid-4th century. == Overview == The Kofun is located along the eastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a slope extending westward from Mount Ryuo. The {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe-no-Michi|qq=}} trail passes near the rear circular portion of the mound, and remnants of ancient stone-paved paths lead toward {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}. It is managed by the Imperial Household Agency, and multiple investigations have been conducted by the agency’s Shoryobu Division since 1971, as well as academic studies in 2016. The keyhole-shaped mound faces westward (downhill). The rear circular section comprises four tiers, and the rectangular front comprises three tiers. Measuring 300 meters in length, the Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun is the largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and the second-largest in Nara Prefecture after the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|lt=Maruyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}} in Kashihara (310 meters). A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun |image =[[File:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (circular rear on the right, rectangular front on the left). |affiliation = {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Shibutani Town, Nara Prefecture{{Sfn|Keikō Emperor Mausoleum Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 3.00 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 50|coordinates = 56.28 |iso = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Length: 300 m
Height: 25 m (rear circular part) built = Mid-to-late 4th century (circa 321–340 AD[[Amano Sueki]] "Considering the Tombs and burials of the Mausoleum" in *The Forefront of Ancient History Research: Mausoleums*, Yōsensha, 2016, pp. 61-62.[https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/koramukodaikaranomemessezi/wanogoozidai/10289.html builts of Giant Kofuns 1 (No. 162)] – Fujidera City Website). |burial = Unknown |burial_person = Designated as Emperor Keikō by the Imperial Household Agency |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (reported) stone pillow |mausoleum = Designated "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" by the Imperial Household Agency |notes = 8th largest Kofun in Japan / 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas in Japan] – Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018.
Largest Kofun from the early Kofun period |map = Japan Nara |map_dot_label = Shibutani Mukoyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Keyhole-shaped mound-west }} '''Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun''' (渋谷向山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|lt=Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Group|qq=}}. While the actual burial figure is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the "Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi" (山邊道上陵), the tomb of Emperor Keikō. This is the 8th largest Kofun in Japan and the 2nd largest in Nara Prefecture, dating back to the mid-to-late 4th century. Recent studies suggest a slightly earlier construction date in the mid-4th century. == Overview == The Kofun is located along the eastern edge of the Nara Basin, on a slope extending westward from Mount Ryuo. The {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe-no-Michi|qq=}} trail passes near the rear circular portion of the mound, and remnants of ancient stone-paved paths lead toward {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}. It is managed by the Imperial Household Agency, and multiple investigations have been conducted by the agency’s Shoryobu Division since 1971, as well as academic studies in 2016. The keyhole-shaped mound faces westward (downhill). The rear circular section comprises four tiers, and the rectangular front comprises three tiers. Measuring 300 meters in length, the Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun is the largest in the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and the second-largest in Nara Prefecture after the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|lt=Maruyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}} in Kashihara (310 meters). A moat surrounds the mound, and several satellite Kofuns have been identified nearby. burial goods recovered include {{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|8=ja|9=円筒埴輪|lt=cylindrical|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figural Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|lt=figural|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figural Haniwa|qq=}} [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a stone pillow Historic Site1 as an Important designation1. == area == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}: * Total length: 300 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 168 meters ** Height: 25 meters ** 4-tier construction * Rectangular front section: ** Width: 170 meters ** Height: 23 meters ** 3-tier construction A moat surrounds the mound, segmented into compartments by earthen bridges. These bridges are believed to have been designed to retain water on sloped terrain. While some sections were modified for agricultural use in modern times, others remain in their original state{{R|Shiraishi}}. == burial goods == [[File:奈良県天理市渋谷出土 石枕.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Stone pillow (Important designation1) traditionally attributed to Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}{{small|On display at the Kansai University Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered during investigations include cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], as well as a notable stone pillow attributed to this mound. The stone pillow, unearthed in 1864, is made of jasper, weighs 24 kilograms, and features incised patterns along its edges. It was Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 in 1961 and is currently housed at the Kansai University Museum in Osaka Prefecture{{Sfn|Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{National Important designation1 Database|201|9838|Stone Pillow}}[http://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/Museum/depot/detail.php?i=7 Kansai University Museum – Stone Pillow]. Another item traditionally linked to this mound is a triangular-rimmed mirror with patterns, now preserved at the Kyoto National Museum. == Associated Tombs == The Imperial Household Agency recognizes three satellite tombs (Historic Site1 "I", "Ro", and "Ha"): * Tomb I ("Uenoyama Kofun"): A 144-meter keyhole-shaped Kofun to the north, potentially preceding Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun. * Tomb Ro ("Maruyama Kofun"): A circular mound with a diameter of approximately 35 meters. * Tomb Ha ("Akasaka Kofun"): A square Kofun measuring approximately 35 meters per side. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kansai University Museum|ja|関西大学博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai University Museum|qq=}} (Suita, Osaka) – Displays the stone pillow (Important designation1). == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/012/index.html Yamanobe-no-Michi-no-E-no-Misasagi] – Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1396495118653.html Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun] – Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|渋谷向山古墳|version=104662227|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yanagimoto Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun]][[sn:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun]] [[ja:渋谷向山古墳]] [[sn:hibutani Mukoyama Kofun]] [[zh:%E6%BE%80%E8%B0%B7%E5%90%91%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Yamato-Aogaki Quasi-National Park]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:柳本古墳群]] {{Commons category|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun}} [[zh:澀谷向山古墳]] {{Commons category|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun}} [[zh:澀谷向山古墳]]
